behavior: Pinned local retrieval preserves reviewed positives, reranks paired hard negatives, and abstains when only a known hard negative is available.
revision_scope: The committed Qwen3 retrieval floor, reranker acceptance floor, manifest validation, production abstention, frozen calibration test, and developer contract.

commands.command: env -u JOBSENTINEL_LIVE_KEYRING_TESTS cargo test -q -p jobsentinel-local-ai --features embedded-ml pinned_qwen3_ranks_all_frozen_requirement_hard_negatives -- --ignored
commands.environment: JOBSENTINEL_QWEN3_TEST_CACHE named an explicit private local test cache; its path is intentionally not recorded.
commands.relevant_result: The one focused model-backed test passed in 325.27 seconds. All three positives and paired hard negatives survived dense retrieval, complete two-candidate reranker output ranked each positive first, production pair matching selected each positive, and production hard-negative-only matching abstained with an unmatched requirement and overall score 0.0.
commands.caveat: The test is opt-in and ignored by default. It covers three synthetic requirement-level pairs on one local platform, not broad calibration.

commands.command: env -u JOBSENTINEL_LIVE_KEYRING_TESTS cargo test -q -p jobsentinel-local-ai --features embedded-ml --lib
commands.relevant_result: All 66 default local-AI library tests passed; 10 opt-in model tests remained ignored.
commands.caveat: The ignored calibration was run separately with the explicit verified cache.

commands.command: env -u JOBSENTINEL_LIVE_KEYRING_TESTS cargo clippy -q -p jobsentinel-local-ai --features embedded-ml --lib -- -D warnings; env -u JOBSENTINEL_LIVE_KEYRING_TESTS cargo check -q -p jobsentinel --features embedded-ml
commands.relevant_result: Strict local-AI Clippy and the embedded-ML desktop compile passed.
commands.caveat: No Tauri process, native dialog, credential backend, or signed build ran.

commands.command: npm run lint:docs; npm run lint:architecture; npm run lint:file-size; npm run lint:security; git diff --check
commands.relevant_result: Docs, harness, local links, prose, architecture, hard file limits, security sensors, secret scanning, and diff whitespace passed.
commands.caveat: The existing cohesive manifest owner reached the non-blocking modularization review threshold; every governed hard limit passed.

commands.command: Read-only Adversarial Review of the settled pinned Qwen3 calibration
commands.relevant_result: The final verdict was APPROVE with no blocker after corrections for complete candidate proof, cross-platform margin, score drift detection, and hard-negative-only abstention.
commands.caveat: The review was static apart from the supplied focused verification and preserves the release matrix as an explicit gap.

tdd_evidence: The original 0.65 production retrieval floor returned no match for a frozen reviewed positive.
A 0.40 candidate floor excluded a paired hard negative before reranking, so it could not prove hard-negative discrimination.
Adversarial review showed that retrieval without a post-rerank acceptance floor would mark a lone hard negative as matched.
The final 0.30 retrieval floor and 3.0 reranker acceptance floor passed pairwise selection and hard-negative-only abstention through production matching.

decisions.retrieval: Use a distinct 0.30 candidate-retrieval floor. Keep retrieval bounded by top-K and separate from the unchanged weak, medium, and strong score bands.
decisions.acceptance: Require a raw resume-requirement reranker score of at least 3.0 before a retrieved candidate becomes a match.
decisions.abstention: A top-ranked candidate below acceptance remains unmatched. Ranking first is never sufficient by itself.
decisions.default_lane: Model download and the pinned calibration remain opt-in. Deterministic exact matching remains available without model files.

remaining_findings: Three synthetic pairs are seed evidence, not production-quality calibration.
Larger reviewed datasets and other query kinds remain required.
Windows 11, macOS 26 and later, and Linux model runs remain release-matrix work.
Modest-hardware memory, storage, and latency budgets remain Gate 4 work.
The weak, medium, and strong score bands are manifest contracts but are not current matcher classifications.
